steps.title: List the bean problems
steps.content: The passage names four barriers: soluble fiber and raffinose make black beans hard to digest, while tannins and trypsin inhibitors interfere with nutrient extraction.

steps.title: Support both parts of the claim
steps.content: The best finding must show fermentation reducing those barriers. It says fermented beans have less soluble fiber and raffinose, and cooked fermented beans show reduced tannins and trypsin inhibitors. A increases antinutrients, C names microorganisms without proving the claimed effect, and D points to cooking rather than fermentation. The answer is B.
